```json
{
  "domain_analysis": {
    "business_context": "A logistics platform managing parcel delivery lifecycle from creation through transit to delivery, with weight-based pricing tiers.",
    "bounded_context": "Shipment Management — covers shipment lifecycle (creation, transit, delivery) and weight-based classification for billing.",
    "ubiquitous_language": [
      { "term": "Shipment", "definition": "A parcel delivery unit with a pickup address, delivery address, weight, and a lifecycle state." },
      { "term": "Pickup Address", "definition": "The origin location where the parcel is collected from the sender." },
      { "term": "Delivery Address", "definition": "The destination location where the parcel is delivered to the recipient." },
      { "term": "Shipment State", "definition": "The lifecycle phase of a shipment: Pending, In Transit, or Delivered." },
      { "term": "Pending", "definition": "Initial state after shipment creation; the parcel has not yet been picked up for transit." },
      { "term": "In Transit", "definition": "The parcel has been picked up and is being transported to the delivery address." },
      { "term": "Delivered", "definition": "Terminal state indicating the parcel has been handed off at the delivery address. Irreversible." },
      { "term": "Weight Bracket", "definition": "A classification tier based on parcel weight that determines the pricing category." },
      { "term": "Standard", "definition": "Weight bracket for parcels weighing > 0 kg and <= 10 kg." },
      { "term": "Heavy", "definition": "Weight bracket for parcels weighing > 10 kg and <= 50 kg." },
      { "term": "Freight", "definition": "Weight bracket for parcels weighing > 50 kg and <= 1000 kg." }
    ]
  },

  "business_rules": [
    {
      "id": "BR-1",
      "rule": "A shipment requires both a pickup address and a delivery address at creation time.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "A shipment without both endpoints is structurally invalid — it cannot represent a real-world delivery. This is a data consistency constraint.",
      "testable_assertion": "Any attempt to create a shipment missing a pickup address or delivery address must be rejected."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-2",
      "rule": "Shipment state transitions follow a strict forward-only lifecycle: Pending → In Transit → Delivered.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Backward transitions (e.g., In Transit → Pending) would corrupt the physical reality of parcel movement. Once a parcel is on a truck, it cannot be un-picked-up.",
      "testable_assertion": "A shipment in state In Transit cannot transition to Pending. A shipment in state Delivered cannot transition to any other state."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-3",
      "rule": "Delivered is a terminal state — no further state changes are permitted.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Delivery represents a completed physical handoff. Reversing it would break audit trails, billing, and chain-of-custody integrity.",
      "testable_assertion": "Any state change attempted on a Delivered shipment must be rejected."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-4",
      "rule": "Shipment weight must be strictly greater than zero.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "A zero or negative weight parcel has no physical meaning. It would corrupt billing calculations and violate physical constraints.",
      "testable_assertion": "Creating or updating a shipment with weight <= 0 must be rejected."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-5",
      "rule": "Shipment weight must not exceed 1000 kg.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "The 1000 kg cap is a business policy reflecting current vehicle/infrastructure limits. A VP could raise this limit if the fleet changes (e.g., adding flatbed trucks).",
      "testable_assertion": "Creating or updating a shipment with weight > 1000 kg must be rejected under current policy."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-6",
      "rule": "Weight brackets determine pricing category: Standard (0–10 kg], Heavy (10–50 kg], Freight (50–1000 kg].",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Bracket boundaries are business policy — marketing or finance could adjust thresholds (e.g., move Heavy to 15–60 kg) without corrupting system integrity.",
      "testable_assertion": "A 10 kg parcel is classified Standard. A 10.01 kg parcel is classified Heavy. A 50 kg parcel is classified Heavy. A 50.01 kg parcel is classified Freight."
    }
  ],

  "acceptance_criteria": [
    {
      "id": "AC-1",
      "given": "A request to create a shipment with a valid pickup address and delivery address",
      "when": "The shipment is created",
      "then": "The shipment is persisted in Pending state with both addresses recorded"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-2",
      "given": "A request to create a shipment missing a pickup address",
      "when": "The creation is attempted",
      "then": "The system rejects the request with a validation error"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-3",
      "given": "A request to create a shipment missing a delivery address",
      "when": "The creation is attempted",
      "then": "The system rejects the request with a validation error"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-4",
      "given": "A shipment in Pending state",
      "when": "It transitions to In Transit",
      "then": "The state is updated to In Transit and the transition is recorded"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-5",
      "given": "A shipment in In Transit state",
      "when": "A transition to Pending is attempted",
      "then": "The system rejects the transition and the shipment remains In Transit"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-6",
      "given": "A shipment in Delivered state",
      "when": "Any state transition is attempted (to Pending or In Transit)",
      "then": "The system rejects the transition and the shipment remains Delivered"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-7",
      "given": "A shipment with weight 0 kg or negative",
      "when": "Creation is attempted",
      "then": "The system rejects the request with a validation error indicating weight must be positive"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-8",
      "given": "A shipment with weight 1001 kg",
      "when": "Creation is attempted",
      "then": "The system rejects the request with a validation error indicating weight exceeds the 1000 kg limit"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-9",
      "given": "A shipment with weight 7 kg",
      "when": "The weight bracket is calculated",
      "then": "The shipment is classified as Standard"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-10",
      "given": "A shipment with weight 25 kg",
      "when": "The weight bracket is calculated",
      "then": "The shipment is classified as Heavy"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-11",
      "given": "A shipment with weight 75 kg",
      "when": "The weight bracket is calculated",
      "then": "The shipment is classified as Freight"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-12",
      "given": "A shipment with weight exactly 10 kg",
      "when": "The weight bracket is calculated",
      "then": "The shipment is classified as Standard (boundary: 0–10 kg inclusive)"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-13",
      "given": "A shipment with weight exactly 50 kg",
      "when": "The weight bracket is calculated",
      "then": "The shipment is classified as Heavy (boundary: 10–50 kg inclusive)"
    }
  ],

  "edge_cases": [
    { "scenario": "Weight exactly at bracket boundary (10 kg)", "expected_behavior": "Classified as Standard. The spec says '0-10 kg is standard', implying 10 kg is included in Standard." },
    { "scenario": "Weight exactly at bracket boundary (50 kg)", "expected_behavior": "Classified as Heavy. The spec says '10-50 kg is heavy', implying 50 kg is included in Heavy." },
    { "scenario": "Transition from Pending directly to Delivered (skipping In Transit)", "expected_behavior": "Spec is silent on this. Likely should be rejected — a parcel cannot be delivered without first being in transit. Flagged as ambiguity." },
    { "scenario": "Weight is a fractional value (e.g., 0.001 kg)", "expected_behavior": "Should be accepted — weight is positive. However, practical minimum weight is unspecified." },
    { "scenario": "Shipment created with weight exactly 1000 kg", "expected_behavior": "Should be accepted — 'cannot exceed 1000 kg' means 1000 kg is the maximum valid weight. Classified as Freight." },
    { "scenario": "Pickup address and delivery address are identical", "expected_behavior": "Spec is silent. Likely a data quality issue but not explicitly prohibited." },
    { "scenario": "Changing weight after shipment is In Transit or Delivered", "expected_behavior": "Spec is silent on weight mutability after creation. If weight drives billing, changes after In Transit could corrupt invoices." }
  ],

  "business_impact": {
    "financial": "Weight bracket classification directly drives revenue — misclassification means under- or over-charging. A 50 kg parcel misclassified as Freight instead of Heavy would overcharge the customer; the reverse would lose revenue.",
    "operational": "State lifecycle enforcement prevents operational chaos (e.g., re-dispatching already-delivered parcels). Weight limits ensure parcels fit vehicle capacity constraints.",
    "user": "Clear state transitions give senders and receivers accurate tracking. Correct weight classification sets price expectations at booking time.",
    "risk": "Primary risks: (1) bracket boundary bugs causing billing disputes, (2) state machine gaps allowing illegal transitions that corrupt tracking data, (3) weight mutability after transit enabling billing fraud.",
    "stakeholders_affected": [
      { "group": "Senders (Shippers)", "impact": "Pricing accuracy at booking; trust that shipment status reflects reality." },
      { "group": "Recipients", "impact": "Accurate delivery tracking; confidence that 'Delivered' means actually delivered." },
      { "group": "Finance / Billing", "impact": "Revenue accuracy depends on correct weight bracket assignment; terminal Delivered state triggers invoicing." },
      { "group": "Operations / Dispatch", "impact": "State machine integrity prevents double-dispatch or re-routing of completed shipments." },
      { "group": "Customer Support", "impact": "Clear lifecycle states reduce ambiguity in dispute resolution." }
    ]
  },

  "confidence": {
    "level": 72,
    "reasoning": "The spec clearly defines the state machine (with one gap), weight constraints, and bracket tiers. However, several business-critical details are unspecified: whether Pending→Delivered is valid, whether weight is mutable after creation, what constitutes a valid address, and exact bracket boundary inclusivity (the '0-10' / '10-50' / 'over 50' language creates overlap at 10 and 50). These ambiguities could cause real billing and operational bugs if guessed wrong.",
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "Forward-only state transitions (Pending → In Transit → Delivered)",
      "Delivered is terminal and irreversible",
      "Weight must be positive and capped at 1000 kg",
      "Three weight brackets exist for pricing"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Whether Pending → Delivered (skipping In Transit) is a valid transition",
      "Exact boundary inclusivity: does 10 kg belong to Standard or Heavy?",
      "Whether weight can be modified after shipment creation",
      "Address validation requirements beyond 'must exist'",
      "Whether additional states (e.g., Cancelled, Failed Delivery, Returned) are needed"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "Bracket boundaries are inclusive on the upper end: Standard includes 10 kg, Heavy includes 50 kg",
      "Pending → Delivered without passing through In Transit should be disallowed",
      "Weight is immutable after shipment creation (not stated but implied by billing dependency)",
      "Address validation is handled elsewhere — only presence is required at this level"
    ]
  },

  "escalations": [
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "The spec does not clarify whether a shipment can transition directly from Pending to Delivered, skipping In Transit. In physical logistics this is invalid, but some business models (e.g., same-location pickup/drop-off, instant fulfillment) may allow it.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Operations / Dispatch", "Recipients", "Finance / Billing"],
      "options": [
        "A: Enforce strict linear lifecycle (Pending → In Transit → Delivered only)",
        "B: Allow Pending → Delivered as a shortcut for edge cases"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option A — enforce strict linear lifecycle. It matches physical reality and prevents accidental state corruption. If a shortcut is needed later, it can be added as an explicit fast-track with audit logging."
    },
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "Weight bracket boundaries overlap in the spec language: '0-10 kg is standard, 10-50 kg is heavy'. Does a 10 kg parcel fall in Standard or Heavy? Same issue at 50 kg.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Senders (Shippers)", "Finance / Billing"],
      "options": [
        "A: Standard is (0, 10], Heavy is (10, 50], Freight is (50, 1000] — upper-inclusive",
        "B: Standard is (0, 10), Heavy is [10, 50), Freight is [50, 1000] — lower-inclusive"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option A — upper-inclusive brackets. '0-10 kg is standard' most naturally reads as 'up to and including 10 kg'. This also favors the customer (lower tier at boundary), reducing billing disputes."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "The spec is silent on weight mutability after shipment creation. If weight can be changed after a shipment is In Transit or Delivered, it could invalidate the billing bracket retroactively.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Finance / Billing", "Customer Support"],
      "options": [
        "A: Weight is immutable after creation",
        "B: Weight is mutable until In Transit, then locked",
        "C: Weight is always mutable with re-billing"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option B — weight is mutable while Pending (allows correction before dispatch), locked once In Transit. This balances operational flexibility with billing integrity."
    }
  ],

  "rejection_reasons": []
}
```
